_comment: Example AIP Credential Token. The token is a compact JWT (header.payload.signature). The decoded form is shown here for readability. In production, agents present the compact JWT string to relying parties. Signatures are placeholders.

aip_chain: <compact JWT for Principal Token at delegation_depth=0 — see delegation-chain.json for decoded form>

_notes.ttl: exp - iat = 3600 seconds in this example. The effective TTL cap is the lowest ttl_max_seconds value across requested scopes in the synced AIP Scope Catalog.
_notes.jti: The issuer agent must keep jti globally unique for its AID across all delegation chains, principals, grants, scopes, and audiences.
_notes.aip_chain: The aip_chain array contains compact JWT strings. Each JWT encodes one Principal Token per principal-token.schema.json. Index 0 is always the root delegation from the human principal.
_notes.aip_principal_assertion: Tier 3 Credential Tokens carry a signed enterprise IdP assertion whose sub matches the root Principal Token principal.id.
_notes.iss_equals_sub: iss equals sub and both identify the acting leaf agent. Validation Step 5g and Step 8 Post-Check B enforce this for both direct and delegated agent-issued Credential Tokens.
_notes.validation: Relying parties validate this token using the Credential Token Validation Algorithm (Steps 1-12, including conditional sub-steps when applicable). Early structural, issuer, chain, revocation, scope, and grant-tier checks are registry-verifiable; Step 10 (DPoP) and Step 11 (mTLS) are relying-party infrastructure.
